text: I *think* you have a script to interact with the workflows? perhaps we could simply make the script detect when ~/.agents/homes/chief-of-staff/workflows is NOT a symlink and give you a fat warning saying "yo, maybe move your workflows to the repo?" [...] 2. **The nudge is exactly right, and yes — there's already a script (`scripts/workflows.py`)**, so I put the check there rather than inventing a new mechanism. It now runs on every invocation and fires a loud stderr warning ("⚠️ workflows/ is a plain directory, not a symlink into a git repo...") whenever `workflows/` exists but isn't a symlink, and stays silent once it is one.
